title: cladomyza microphylla
intro: Cladomyza microphylla, also known as the bushy cladomyza, is a shrub native to Australia, belonging to the family Cunoniaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in Australian folk medicine, with its leaves and stems exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Cladomyza microphylla has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating rheumatic pain, and treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and its leaves and stems can also be used in topical applications for skin treatments.
